name: Chuck Wayne
abstract: Chuck Wayne (February 27, 1923 – July 29, 1997) was an American jazz guitarist. He came to prominence in the 1940s, and  was among the earliest jazz guitarists to play in the bebop style.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: that was the beginning of the George Shearing Quintet — the original George Shearing Quintet — which was Denzel Best, Margie Hyams, Chuck Wayne, George Shearing and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: We’ve got Denzil Best, Al McKibbon, Chuck Wayne, George Shearing, and myself. Traveling with us was George’s wife. Chuck Wayne’s wife was traveling with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Spinozza
about_quotes.evidence: I certainly listened to a lot of jazz players: Wes Montgomery, Joe Pass, people like that, you know, Chuck Wayne. I knew all these guitarists. I knew who they were.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Spinozza interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, NY, September 27, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: "the record that got me started, “Changing With the Times” — Margie — who was it, Chuck Wayne, Margie Hyams, that band."
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: Mundell Lowe, Billy Bauer, Chuck Wayne, Tony Mottola, Al Caiola — all great players, playing three chords.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] So Chuck Wayne was the original guitarist. So he’s the one who went in… [INTERVIEWEE] No.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
